comment.body: It’s ironic that the court declaring his executive orders on tariffs exceeded his authority (the ITC) is part of the executive branch. It’s an administrative tribunal — so these judges actually “work for” Trump. The appeal from the ITC will go to an Article III court in DC (US Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit), which isn’t likely to be a more receptive forum than the ITC. But to some extent, that will depend on the composition of the panel.  What a mess.
comment.raw_body: It’s ironic that the court declaring his executive orders on tariffs exceeded his authority (the ITC) is part of the executive branch. It’s an administrative tribunal — so these judges actually “work for” Trump. The appeal from the ITC will go to an Article III court in DC (US Court of Appeals for the Federal Circuit), which isn’t likely to be a more receptive forum than the ITC. But to some extent, that will depend on the composition of the panel.  What a mess.
